coreJobStatement: Manufacture rotating electrical machines that reliably convert between mechanical and electrical energy at specified power, voltage, frequency, and efficiency ratings [SRC: VN_generator_keybars]
productJobStatement: Help Stator Structural Frame to anchor silicon steel lamination segments axially, maintain stator slot geometry within design tolerances, and resist cyclic 2x line-frequency electromagnetic forcing (100-120 Hz) over a 30-year machine operating lifetime
anchorLevel: L4 (subsystem component — keybar profile within stator structural frame)
lPath: L7 Electrical Machine Manufacturing Ecosystem > L6b Stator Core Assembly > L5 Stator Structural Frame > L4 Keybar Profiles

segments.name: Turbo-generator OEM (large)
segments.qualifier: GE Vernova, Siemens Energy, Alstom, Hitachi Energy
segments.characteristics: Building 12-72 keybar machines with hydrogen or water cooling; ISO/IEC type testing required
segments.targetabilityReason: Highest precision demand; longest machine life; strictest qualification
segments.circumstance: Building 12-72 keybar machines with hydrogen or water cooling; ISO/IEC type testing required
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — highest precision demand; longest machine life; strictest qualification

segments.name: Hydro-generator OEM
segments.characteristics: Large-diameter slow-speed machines; high keybar count; less standardised cross-sections
segments.targetabilityReason: Higher keybar volume per machine; large-format tolerances
segments.circumstance: Large-diameter slow-speed machines; high keybar count; less standardised cross-sections
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — higher keybar volume per machine; large-format tolerances

segments.name: Industrial motor OEM
segments.characteristics: Continuous-duty motors; standardised frame sizes; some geometry repeat
segments.circumstance: Continuous-duty motors; standardised frame sizes; some geometry repeat
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — higher run volumes; price competition

segments.name: Stator rewind contractor
segments.characteristics: Replacing worn or damaged keybars in service; must match original geometry exactly
segments.targetabilityReason: No design flexibility; die re-qualification required
segments.circumstance: Replacing worn or damaged keybars in service; must match original geometry exactly
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — no design flexibility; die re-qualification required

alternatives.name: CNC-machined from alloy bar (in-house or job shop)
alternatives.tradeoffs: 50-70% material waste; high per-unit machining cost; in-house capital and programming burden; no die lead time; full geometry flexibility [SRC: JTBD_generator_keybars]

alternatives.name: Cold-drawn near-net-shape profile (ZOLLERN path)
alternatives.tradeoffs: 6-12 wk die lead time for new geometry; 100 kg minimum order; ~40-70% machining waste eliminated; IT8 tolerance; requires OEM first-article qualification [SRC: JTBD_generator_keybars]

alternatives.name: Standard catalog steel profile stock
alternatives.tradeoffs: Limited geometry options; may require significant secondary machining to meet dovetail spec; no custom geometry; off-the-shelf delivery

alternatives.name: Welded / fabricated keybar
alternatives.tradeoffs: Lower dimensional accuracy; stress concentrations at welds; HAZ fatigue risk; maintenance/retrofit only; not viable for new OEM build

alternatives.name: Do nothing / continue in-house machining (status quo)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero switching cost; zero new qualification burden; full geometry control; tradeoff: high material waste, in-house capacity tied up

jobSteps.description: Define keybar cross-section geometry, alloy, tolerance class, length, surface roughness, and EN 10204 3.1 cert requirement
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Keybar spec is the first constraint; geometry error cascades through lamination tooling
jobSteps.rawStatement: Define keybar cross-section geometry, alloy, tolerance class, length, surface roughness, and EN 10204 3.1 cert requirement
jobSteps.rationale: Keybar geometry specification is the first constraint; errors cascade through lamination tooling

jobSteps.description: Identify and qualify keybar suppliers for required geometry; verify stock or confirm die availability
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Supplier qualification, lead time, and geometry availability determine schedule
jobSteps.rawStatement: Identify and qualify keybar suppliers for required geometry; verify stock or confirm die availability
jobSteps.rationale: Supplier qualification, lead time, and geometry availability determine stator assembly schedule

jobSteps.description: Receive and incoming-inspect keybar bars; cut to stator core length; verify alloy cert
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Dimensional compliance and cert chain must be verified before assembly begins
jobSteps.rawStatement: Receive and incoming-inspect keybar bars; cut to stator core length; verify alloy cert
jobSteps.rationale: Dimensional compliance and material traceability must be confirmed before assembly

jobSteps.description: CMM-verify keybar geometry against tolerance envelope; confirm alloy traceability
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Keybars are geometry-critical — misfit discovered post-installation is extremely costly
jobSteps.rawStatement: CMM-verify keybar geometry against tolerance envelope; confirm alloy traceability to EN 10204 3.1
jobSteps.rationale: Misfit discovered post-installation requires stator disassembly — extremely costly

jobSteps.description: Install keybars into stator frame slots; stack lamination segments; apply axial clamping
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Install fit depends entirely on keybar geometry accuracy
jobSteps.rawStatement: Install keybars into stator frame slots; stack lamination segments; apply axial clamping
jobSteps.rationale: Install fit depends entirely on keybar geometry accuracy

jobSteps.description: Monitor stator core dimensions and slot alignment during lamination stacking; El-CID core test
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Keybar geometry deviation shows up in progressive slot misalignment during stacking
jobSteps.rawStatement: Monitor stator core dimensions and slot alignment during lamination stacking
jobSteps.rationale: Keybar geometry deviation shows up in progressive slot misalignment during stacking
